description: Use this API to return an array of character values for subsystem buses. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.2.0. ierr, carray = alodbuschar(sid, flag, string)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem buses to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: An array of NSTR elements specifying NSTR of the following strings indicating the bus and/or load quantities desired. (input; no default allowed).

return_values.name: carray
return_values.description: An array of NSTR*DIM elements specifying Is an array of at least NSTR columns, each of which contains DIM elements. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

flag.description: for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3) with at least one in-service load.
for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3), including those with only out- of-service loads.
for all in-service buses, including those that are not load buses.
for all load buses, including those with only out-of-service loads.
for all buses, including those that are not load buses.
for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3) with at least one in-service scalable load.
for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3) with at least one scalable load, including those with all such loads out-of-service.
for all load buses with at least one scalable load, including those with all such loads out-of-service.
for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3) with at least one in-service fixed load.
for only in-service load buses (type code 1, 2 or 3) with at least one fixed load, including those with all such loads out-of-service.
for all load buses with at least one fixed load, including those with all such loads out-of-service.

error_codes.description: DIM, and hence the size of CARRAY, is not large enough.
